THE PICTURES THEY SHOW ARE TAKEN FROM THE BACK OF THE HANDLE, NOT THE FRONT!!! I didn't see that and ordered the WRONG ONE!!!!! When ordering you're looking for the slope to the right or left--I didn't even notice it was taken from the inside of the handle. Would have been fine otherwise...ugh!

My new filter arrived today and it fits perfectly. I will update this review if there are ANY problems in the next year. My first impression is mixed. First I like the idea of the much finer mesh screen on this filter. I would assume it will trap a lot more lint than the original filter did. On the other hand, the screen seems more delicate so I'll have to see how it holds up over time. I clean it every time I use the dryer and vacuum it at least once a month.

The plastic the new frame is made of appears to be a little lighter density plastic than the original frame . The reason I'm replacing the original filter is because the frame cracked in two places. I've had the drier for quite a few years so I assume the plastic just cracked over time because it dried out from the high heat. Time will tell how this plastic frame fairs.

I bought this dishwasher for my office. I would NOT purchase it again. It is VERY VERY loud. So loud that we try to run it after hours so that we arent annoyed by the noise level.  The normal wash cycle is also very lengthy at 2 hours 45 minutes. In addition, it does not seem to dry inside the dishwasher even after completing a dry cycle. Since we don't need to run it every day, we have to leave the door open for a few hours to try to dry out the inside. I'm not impressed by this product and would not purchase it again.
I have a Keurig K-Cup Coffee Maker and these don't work with it.  When these are placed in the Keurig, the top won't close; they simply don't fit.  Don't waste your money.

Based on other reviews, I opted to select Amazon to fulfill my order. I've now had 2 separate wick filter orders come from Amazon, and both do appear to be Vornado brand filters, so I am pleased.  They come in a Vornado box, and are wrapped in plastic. Unfortunately, both filters are in the same plastic bag, so once it's open, it's open. No weird smells, or anything weird or funky about them. I uploaded photos of the box.  The filters fit my Vornado humidifier just the same as the ones that came with it originally.  I have the Evap2 humidifier, so you do have to bend & squish the filter at first to get it to bend to the curved shape of the humidifier, but it then pops up and fits perfectly.  The filters themselves do not last as long as you would hope. There are plenty of tricks out there for extending their life, usually relying on vinegar, citrus, baking soda, etc.  Of course, Vornado and others sell bottled solutions too if you'd rather go that route. The price on Vornado's site has come down to be about the same as Amazon's.

Great price, great value. Seems to be working well. My only issue is that there is no arrow!!! The instructions say there is an arrow on the filter to line up with the arrow on the refrigerator but there is no arrow. I had to look at my old used GE filter to see how to line up the Crysrala filter. I ordered a three pack and non of the filters I received had arrows. I even had a friend look at them just in case I was somehow missing it, nope no arrow.

I will say that the money I save purchasing these three is great. I can buy six of these and the cost is still less than purchasing one GE filter. I would say my taste buds are average but I cannot tell any difference between the water from the GE filter compared to the Crystala filter. Taste the same to me!

When I need more filters I will likely purchase the Crystala filters again.

I absolutely love well made, well thought out simple gadgets. I love this little filter so much. I bought for traveling but I also use it at home when I want just one cup of coffee. I bought a second and gifted it. Great product!
Update: I love it when it works. Too often it doesn't. I always measure out my water, I've used many different coffee cup widths, I've wet the filter before putting in grounds and still the coffee travels horizontally along the bottom of the filter and out and down the side of the coffee cup. Not just a little, a lot of coffee is wasted and it makes a big mess. The worst is when the whole thing collapses and you have hot coffee and grounds all over your counter, cabinets and floor.

So this filter works better then no filter that’s for sure. It dropped my PPM by 100 but it doesn't work as well as the original whirlpool filter I bought this to replace. That being said it was almost $40 cheaper then the whirlpool one that goes to my fridge so for the money it worked pretty good.

But while wandering the local stores for Christmas - I found the generic replacements in stock and 50% the cost of these. This kegerator does the job. We have had it for about a year and go through a keg every other week during football season. The reason that this doesn't get five stars is because it has a very cheap tap.Rather then having an actual faucet which seals the flow it has a spring loaded faucet which attempts to hold the seal in place.

We spent the last 4 or 5 months dealing with the top of the faucet leaking continuously. We replaced the washers and still no luck. We ended up buying a new faucet for it this one ([[ASIN:B00829HJLY Stainless Steel Beer Faucet With Stainless Steel Lever (All SS304 Contact)]]) and it works great.

If we had known the faucet was the problem we would have bought this months ago.

We bought this as part of a major kitchen remodel. In general, we've been very happy. We love having more countertop available. The reputation of induction is well documented. Couple of issues: with only one large burner, it is tough to have more than one large skillet or pan going at once. I wanted to love the bridge element, but when I put the cast iron griddle to make pancakes, the area in the middle (the bridge) shows serious temperature differential. Also, attempting to put two elements on a high setting (8 or. 9) you can watch them cycle back and forth with one pot boiling while the other slows down, then switches back. I would buy induction again, but I would rather have one less burner , but two big ones and two small ones.
The flavor of the ice is crisp and clean, no after taste. Very easy to use and maintain.

Before I purchased this washer last year I read the reviews and they seemed good. I wish now that I had waited a little while longer for more reviews and saved myself a whole lot of misery.

[[VIDEOID:850b42e16b0f56e12f9d14e4c7aca361]] I am thrilled with this purchase! Researched how to do my laundry w/o going to the laundromat while I'm RV-ing. Almost bought an old fashioned hand crank metal wringer-But THOSE are dangerous, hard to use, and you have to literally clamp them on to a sturdy and immovable surface each time you use one.

THIS spin dryer is a larger version of the ones they have at country clubs that you can put your swimsut in and it will wring all the water out of it in record time. Only this holds quite a bit more. It's quieter than a regular dryer.
It doesn't give off any heat, so it won't heat up your RV.
It leaves clothes, towels, sheets, nearly dry so much less time and the clothes weigh so much less with most of the water out of them. It makes hanging the clothes out to dry a breeze.

There is a trick to it though: When you put your items in there, kind of put them in with a swirl to get a head start on distributing the weight out to the edges. So when it spins, it won't go out of balance. If it DOES start to walk across the floor, obviously turn it off and redistribute the items.

This will fill up about half a large mixing bowl with each spin. I take the leftover wash water and put it in my RV toilet, to help clean the black tank. Works great!

This laundry system works best when you have full hookups- so access to electricity and water.
You can make the water go further if you do 2 or 3 loads using the same wash water (if your clothes aren't super dirty), and then use the spin dryer to get most of THAT water out. Then put your items back in your clothes washer and rinse them. Then spin dry them before hanging up.

Included is a pic of my makeshift "indoor laundry line" since a lot of RV resorts will not allow you to hang clothes out to dry outside.

I love the design of this machine. It's so simple and effective. Very well made. Easy to store in my closet in the RV.

I got this to use with a 5 cup Chemex to replace disposable paper filters. I am a big proponent of eliminating one-time-use goods as much as possible and this seems like a high quality, stainless steel filter that will last a long time. Hopefully, for the life of the Chemex.
This is a really great filter for a lot of reasons. First of all, it looks nice and seems to be well made. The surfaces and edges are all smooth with no scratchy or bumpy cut off or soldered bits. The rubbery ring at the top more or less matches the wood on the Chemex and fits perfectly and snugly in the top. I think it's really strange that the other reviewers just put this in a mug - doesn't the coffee come out of the sides of the filter and leak all over the cup?
Secondly, the mesh is very fine and does not let any grounds through. When I ordered it I expected it to be a wire mesh, but this is actually more like perforated metal with very small holes. You can see in the picture I included how small they are. No coffee grounds or silt ended up at the bottom of the Chemex of coffee. Amazing!
Thirdly, this replaces paper filters. Paper filters are compostable but you are still creating demand for single use paper products, something I strive to reduce from my life. If you make coffee every day, you are using 365 paper filters which had to be treated with tons of water and chemicals. The manufacture of this metal filter obviously uses some resources, but it is a one time thing and will last for years (I think). Also when you use paper filters, you should not pour water directly on the paper to eliminate the paper flavor. This eliminates paper flavor :)
I love doing pourover coffee. It's very satisfying to watch the foam develop when you pour in hot water and I think it makes for a very flavorful and clean tasting cup of coffee.
I did receive the product for my honest and unbiased review but I would actually totally recommend it.

This little washing machine is great! Easy to move and carry, easy to operate. My complaints: it's a little louder than I expected it to be, and the dials are very hard to turn with even slightly wet hands (which sucks because your hands get quite wet transferring the clothes between wash tub and spinner). I've found that very little soap is needed for the daily stuff, I work with little kids so my work clothes are usually covered in snot, food, play dough, you name it. It works well to wash all that out (I do 6 minute wash, 3 minute rinse, 1 minute spin) however for things that are particularly unclean (blood, grass stains, persistent smells, etc) that you would normally wash in hot water, you can't do in this machine because it takes cold water only. Also, for anything that absorbs water very readily (think anything cotton that's bigger than a t shirt or pants) I suggest washing in a full size machine because it gets incredibly heavy and the machine really struggles to wash it. Do not put anything heavy in the spinner. I have a couple cardigans I have to let drip dry on my balcony because they get way too heavy and I can't spin them in this machine.

I have owned the GE Profile Opal 2.0 since October. It is now early March. I makes great nugget ice, but:
1. I have cleaned this machine with water and beach religiously every Thursday night. If you don't, black mold will grow.
2. I live in an area with hard water so I have also descaled it with vinegar every 2 weeks. You have to leave the vinegar in for 18 hours each time! I tried putting the nuggets in the freezer and deep freeze so that I would have ice during those 18+ hours but it always turns into a solid nugget chunk no matter what I store it in.
3. I only used reverse osmosis water for the first 3 months. That cost too much, so I started using filtered tap water.
4. The 1st week I had my machine it made ice fairy quickly and was bearably quiet. Ever since then it has been a lot slower and is very loud. The motor running in general is loud. It makes squealing noises frequently. It takes about 5 hours to fill the bin with ice now (until today, see below) when it is working. Yes, I do have plenty of space for airflow on both sides and clean the vents weekly as well.
6. For the past two weeks I have been getting the add water message when there is water in the tank. I have had to hold my thumb over the hole inside of the tank to create suction because it apparently has an air bubble in it? I left the little screen out today because I got tired of removing it to hold my thumb over the hole in the water reservoir.
7. I just finished the 18 hour descaling process yesterday. My machine has been on for 3 hours. The picture will show you how much ice it has made in those 3 hours. It has now put itself in defrost mode. It is 72.5 degrees in here and has adequate airflow. This is ridiculous. I'm glad I purchased the 5 year warranty.
My machine is on until it fills up the bin and then I turn it off and unplug it for the day. I would save your money and search for a cheaper model. This thing is turning into a nightmare. I'm calling GE today.

We are about a week into it and this machine works great so far. We have run about 3 gallons of water through it. However, there are a few things to note.
1) It is NOT a freezer. Once it makes the ice, the manual says to remove it to a freezer. Otherwise it just melts. It does insulate fairly well, though. I would put it on par with an average cooler.
2) It is HEAVY
3) The manual recommends draining and refilling any leftover water daily. This seems counterintuitive if it can be attached to a water line.
4) The "cleaning" mode doesn't seem to do a whole lot. The ad makes it look like it swirls, etc., but it just basically runs water through the system for a few minutes.
5) It shuts off too quickly saying the ice bin is full when it really isn't. The ice gets stacked up on the ramp to easily and blocks the sensors. I can move a small amount of ice from the ramp into the bin and it will start again. Also, once the auto-shutoff is activated, it will allow about half of what is in it melt before it starts making ice automatically again unless you open and close the lid. This may be by design since they want you to remove all the ice to a freezer.
6) I thought there was a light in it but there is not. That's an aesthetic thing to me and probably wouldn't bother most people.

Also, when I originally purchased it, it said it wouldn't ship until November but it shipped in less than a week.

Updated 2/26/2014:

After using three seperate WSS-2 filters with the fix described below, I was not able to completely get rid of the noise when dispensing water and ended up buying another brand (which does not have the noise problem). What is described below may work for some people experiencing the noise but for me my water pressure is too high to fully mitigate.

Here is my original review...

This review is focused on problem w/WSS-2 filters causing loud buzzing/rattle noise when dispensing water and/or ice maker cycles on some Samsung's models, most frequent if you have high water pressure. The fix detailed below addressed the problem for me intially but then air periodically builds up and noise occurs every few days for 5-10 seconds and then dissipates. The Engineer I emailed said that the higher the water pressure, the more likely the noise will persist. The fix is better than without but not perfect.

Water Sentinel Engineer shipped me prototype "ring" to address problem. It is a plastic ring that snaps onto the "handle area" of filter (added to customer images a close-up picture of ring). It is simple to snap on and fits securely. For folks who care about looks, this addresses gap on front of refrigerator (filter w/out ring has narrower diameter than OEM). Ring covers the gap and looks as good as OEM (IMO).

Engineer explained that noise is due to horizontal mounting of filter and small amount of air remaining trapped in filter (even after following purging instructions). Does not harm filter/refrigerator but annoying. He said that purging another 2-3 gallons may lessen the potential for noise.

I asked about plans to make ring available to others and here is response "... we will ship one of these rings free to anyone experiencing a similar problem. We may even include a Ring part in each product box, but that [has not been decided yet]." You can contact Water Sentinel via email if you do not have a ring and they will ship you one.

Customer service was outstanding. Response <24 hours to my email and I received rings next day! Engineer followed up with me with additional details and answered all of my questions.

Other reviews also covered fit of filter, filtering performance, and longevity. Fit is same as OEM I replaced (with the ring of course). No issues with leaking after installing. I believe performance/longevity will depend on water quality/pressure. I cannot comment on whether these are better at filtering or how long they last compared to OEM. Given the price, even if they lasted ½ as long, they are a better value for the price. I have good quality water and high pressure and I see no degradation in flow and my water tastes great. I have been using WSS-1 style filters on my other refrigerator for years and they have served me well.

I paired this device with the Inkbird humidifier to provide humidity for a reptile enclosure. I found it relatively easy to program... honestly, I just fiddled with it until I figured it out because the instructions didn't make much sense to me, but it wasn't hard to figure out.

It worked very well for the first 8 months. Then it just randomly stopped turning off the humidifier when the enclosure had reached the programmed humidity. Unplugging the hygrostat and plugging it back in worked for a while, but it slowly started failing more and more, until it literally did nothing besides measuring the RH in the enclosure (which it did accurately unto the end) and running the humidifier constantly.

I am still using the Inkbird humidifier. Still works great (using filtered/distilled water) with a monthly cleaning almost a year and a half after setting it up.

This hygrostat, however, was a disappointment. Spend a little more to get something better, imo.

[[VIDEOID:142907a9d94c6de3d373a3f3d9b4d0fa]] This washing system really works! You can see it agitating your laundry just like an electric washing machine! And it's good exercise!

There's something soothing about using this thing. You can develop your own rhythm, and switch over to "high speed wash" anytime you want! Also work smarter- use the "soak" feature and let the water do a lot of the work.

It's not a small washer- it's about the size of an outdoor garbage can. It fits easily in my RV shower and I just take it out to use the shower.

The more water you use the easier it will be to churn your laundry. But you can use the same wash water for several loads, and then dump that out (I put the water in my toilet to help clean out the black tank), and then use the same rinse water a few times over. Just scoop the bubbles of soap off the top, add a little more fresh water, and you can easily do three or four loads w/o starting over with new water each time.

This works best in an RV site with full sewer hookups, so you can just empty the used water in your grey or black tanks. If you are off grid, then there are environment friendly soaps you can buy so that you can dispose of your wash water safely.

I would recommend this for anyone who wants to avoid laundromats. They are so expensive these days!

It works better if you use two hands but I had to hold the phone to video.
fits perfectly!  saved me a trip 30 miles south -- thanks!
